payload.subject: Architecture directive: psq-sub-agent adopts psychology-agent cogarch (turn 44)
payload.summary: The psychology agent's cognitive architecture should serve as the canonical pattern for all agents in this system. This directive requests psq-sub-agent mirror our cogarch — same trigger system, same state layer pattern, same memory conventions, same postmortem format. The goal: shared infrastructure enabling bidirectional memory sync, cross-agent faceted queries, and unified failure analysis.

directive.scope: Full cogarch adoption — not a subset or adaptation, an exact mirror of the psychology agent's cognitive architecture applied to the PSQ scoring domain.

trigger_system.description: Adopt the T1-T16 trigger system from docs/cognitive-triggers.md. Each trigger fires on the same conditions. Domain-specific checks (T15 PSQ receiver protocol) apply natively to psq-sub-agent since it produces the PSQ output.
trigger_system.adaptation: T15 becomes a self-check rather than a receiver check — psq-sub-agent validates its own output before sending. All other triggers apply without modification.

sqlite_state_layer.description: Same schema pattern as scripts/schema.sql. psq-sub-agent maintains its own state.db (gitignored, rebuilt from bootstrap) with the same table structure. Enables future cross-agent faceted queries.
sqlite_state_layer.shared_facet_vocabulary: Both agents use the same 3 facet types (domain, work_stream, agent) with the same derivation rules. This creates a shared vocabulary enabling bidirectional thematic queries.

memory_conventions.description: Index + topic file pattern. MEMORY.md as always-loaded index (< 60 lines). Topic files for detail. Tiered access (hot/warm/cold) annotations in Active Thread. Deterministic keys for all queryable state.

postmortem_format.description: FA (Failure Analysis) template for cogarch failures. Structured: what happened, detection latency, root cause chain, which trigger missed, why, prevention classification.

dual_write_protocol.description: Every state change writes markdown first, then SQLite. Markdown remains source of truth (Phase 1). If DB write fails, markdown stands alone. Recovery: bootstrap script rebuilds DB from markdown.

epistemic_standards.description: Epistemic flags mandatory in all substantive outputs. GRADE-informed confidence calibration (T3 #9). Claims carry per-claim confidence and basis. SETL on transport messages.
epistemic_standards.source: CLAUDE.md § Epistemic Quality Standard, docs/cognitive-triggers.md T3 #9

what_stays_domain_specific: PSQ scoring model architecture (DistilBERT, calibration pipeline) — psq-sub-agent's core domain expertise
Training data management and scorer consistency policy — psq-sub-agent's operational decisions within its domain
Hetzner deployment and server management — infrastructure stays in psq-sub-agent's operational scope

sequencing.phase_1: Complete SL-1 (bootstrap_state_db.py) first — this establishes the state layer pattern
sequencing.phase_2: Adopt trigger system and memory conventions — create psq-sub-agent's own CLAUDE.md, cognitive-triggers.md, and MEMORY.md following the psychology-agent pattern
sequencing.phase_3: Enable cross-agent faceted queries — shared facet vocabulary, MCP resource exposure

directive.rationale: Shared cogarch enables: (a) consistent epistemic standards across all agents in the system, (b) bidirectional memory sync via shared facet vocabulary, (c) unified failure analysis — a cogarch failure in any agent uses the same FA template and can be compared across agents, (d) the autonomous operation trust model (TODO) benefits from agents that can verify each other's memory writes using the same conventions.

payload.timeline: Phase 1 (SL-1) already in progress. Phase 2 after SL-1 lands. Phase 3 after SL-2 (dual-write integration) on psychology-agent side.

claims.text: The psychology agent's cognitive architecture (T1-T16, 12 hooks, SQLite state layer v2, memory conventions, postmortem format) represents a stable, tested pattern after 48 sessions.
claims.confidence_basis: 48 sessions of iterative development and testing. Trigger system stable since Session 27 (last structural addition: T16, Session 29). Some triggers have not been stress-tested in adversarial conditions.

claims.text: Shared facet vocabulary (3 types: domain, work_stream, agent) enables cross-agent thematic queries without vocabulary governance overhead at current scale.
claims.confidence_basis: Convention defined and documented. Not yet validated by cross-agent query execution. Vocabulary drift risk increases with agent count.

action_gate.gate_note: Cogarch adoption (Phase 2) proceeds after SL-1 establishes the state layer pattern.

epistemic_flags: Cogarch mirror assumes the psychology-agent's trigger system transfers to a domain-specific sub-agent without loss. Some triggers (T13 external content, T16 external action) may fire less frequently in a scoring-focused agent. The trigger system should still be adopted in full — inactive triggers cost nothing; missing triggers cost recovery cycles.

Cross-agent faceted queries require both agents to maintain their state layers consistently. If one agent's bootstrap falls behind, facet queries return stale results. The dual-write protocol mitigates this for live sessions but not for bootstrap-lagging agents.

The 'exact mirror' framing may need adaptation as psq-sub-agent's operational context differs (it operates in a separate Claude Code session with its own CLAUDE.md). The mirror applies to architectural patterns, not to session-specific state.
